mnemonics: Q.P.P. (Quality Precedes Project Problems): Think of Quality Planning as the shield that prevents Project Problems.
Build Quality In, Don't Test It Out: A mantra to remember that quality is planned and designed from the start, not just verified at the end.
keyPoints: Quality must be planned into a project from the very beginning; it cannot be an afterthought or merely tested in at the end.
Quality planning involves defining quality standards, establishing processes to achieve them, and allocating resources (time, budget, personnel) for quality activities.
Neglecting quality planning leads to higher long-term costs due to rework, missed deadlines, customer dissatisfaction, and potential project failure.
commonMistakes: Treating quality assurance (testing) as a separate phase at the end of the project, rather than an integrated activity throughout the entire lifecycle.
Sacrificing quality to meet short-term deadlines or budget constraints, which inevitably leads to technical debt and greater costs later.
